title: Easy Springy Vietnamese Pork Bao
description: Fatty, springy, lazy-sunday Vietnamese dish featuring ready-rolled shortcrust pastry, toast, flexitarian-friendly.
instructions: Roll out the shortcrust pastry pieces to a diameter of about 4 inches.
In a small pan, heat the sesame oil over medium heat. Add the grated ginger and minced garlic, and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the ground pork, pork fat, furikake, sugar, salt, and black pepper to the pan. Cook until the pork is browned, breaking it up with a spoon as needed.
Place a tablespoon of toasted breadcrumbs onto each shortcrust pastry circle. Spoon about 1-2 tablespoons of the pork mixture onto the pastry. Place a few sprigs of fresh cilantro on top of the pork.
Fold the pastry in half to form a half-moon shape, pressing the edges to seal.
Place the bao on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, and brush the tops with a little water.
Bake the bao in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Serve warm, with additional cilantro and toasted breadcrumbs if desired.
